findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cross-reference in each of R2/R3/R4's Scope sections noting that the other two stories also modify criteria_routing.json and must not land in the same batch — or designate a merge order.
evidence: R2 Scope: 'criteria_routing.json (new DET entry, advisory)' — sequenced after floor-split only
R3 Scope: 'criteria_routing.json (new advisory criterion)' — sequenced after floor-split only
R4 Scope: 'criteria_routing.json (advisory entry)' — sequenced after floor-split only
Parent Context: 'R2/R3/R4 each touch criteria_routing.json and land serially in any order (file-impact keeps them out of one batch)' — the constraint lives only in the parent
findings.finding: R2, R3, and R4 each modify criteria_routing.json and are described in the parent as landing 'serially in any order,' but none of the three children documents a mutual-exclusion constraint or merge-ordering rule between themselves — the file-conflict risk is acknowledged at the parent level only, not governed within any child.
findings.location: children b080-fa0b-b1ce-4fad (R2), 3a27-f8fa-c417-43db (R3), 03a9-e6d1-4acc-435d (R4) — Scope sections
scenarios: Two of R2/R3/R4 are developed on concurrent branches; both branches modify criteria_routing.json; the second merge creates a conflict or silently drops one entry if reviews don't cross-check the file.
An implementer picks up one of R2/R3/R4 without reading the parent epic, unaware that the other two also touch criteria_routing.json, and merges without coordinating.
findings.suggested_fix: In each of R2, R3, R4 Scope sections, add: 'Note: R2/R3/R4 all modify criteria_routing.json; land serially (not in one batch); confirm the other two are merged or diverged before submitting.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'blocking-FP proxy' metric (signal, time window, computation method) in the plan — or add a pointer to the R7 child AC where it is authoritatively specified — so the 10% demotion trigger is unambiguously evaluable.
evidence: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory' — the proxy metric is unnamed
'blocking-FP proxy' appears three times (Success Criteria, AC4 prose, Context) with no definition of the underlying signal, time window, or computation method
ABSENCE: no reference to a child story, ADR, or existing doc that defines the proxy metric
findings.finding: The 'blocking-FP proxy' metric is used as an operational trigger (>10% threshold → registry demotion) in both the Success Criteria and AC4 but is never defined: no specification of what event or signal constitutes a false-positive, what window 'trailing' covers, or where the rate is computed from. An executor implementing R7 instrumentation cannot instrument the right thing without this definition.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'The gate ships with no blocking-FP regression'; AC4 — 'R7 dogfood monitoring documented'; Context — 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%'
scenarios: Executor writing R7 instrumentation must guess what to record — different interpretations (reviewer-rejected findings, post-claim edit rate, manual audits) produce incompatible metrics that cannot be compared against the 10% threshold
The demotion trigger in AC4 becomes unevaluable if the proxy is not defined before R7 ships
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence in the Context block, e.g.: 'The blocking-FP proxy is defined as: findings with BLOCKING posture that are rejected or demoted by the reviewing engineer within the same session, measured as a 30-day trailing rate per criterion; the R7 child AC is the authoritative definition.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Sharpen AC4's 'R7 dogfood monitoring documented' sub-clause by naming the concrete repo artifact (e.g., a specific section in docs/plan-review-gate.md, or a pointer to the R7 child's committed runbook) that must exist for this sub-clause to be satisfied.
evidence: 'with R7 dogfood monitoring documented' — 'documented' is not tied to a specific file path, section, or repo artifact
The other two sub-clauses in AC4 name concrete artifacts (packaged registry, docs/plan-review-gate.md); the R7 sub-clause does not
findings.finding: AC4 bundles three distinct sub-clauses — (a) rubric changes incorporated with no config-flag, (b) R7 dogfood monitoring 'documented', and (c) docs/plan-review-gate.md updated — but sub-clause (b) does not specify what in-session repo artifact satisfies 'documented': it could mean the R7 instrumentation code is committed, or the monitoring runbook is written, or actual monitoring results are present. Without a concrete artifact named, the AC is not fully evaluable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'All landed criteria/rubric changes are incorporated directly into the packaged registry/rubrics (no config-flag or overlay gating), with R7 dogfood monitoring documented, and docs/plan-review-gate.md updated to match.'
scenarios: Reviewer closing this epic cannot determine whether 'R7 dogfood monitoring documented' is satisfied by the R7 Gerrit change alone, a new doc section, or collected monitoring data
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'with R7 dogfood monitoring documented' with 'with the blocking-FP proxy metric and demotion procedure described in docs/plan-review-gate.md (§ Dogfood Monitoring) as committed by the R7 child story'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the R7-instrumented blocking-FP demotion path: e.g., 'A criteria_routing.json change lowering posture to advisory is demonstrably reachable when R7's blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, with the trigger and precedent documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md.'
evidence: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change' — Success Criteria bullet 2
AC line 4 says 'R7 dogfood monitoring documented' but does not verify the 10%-threshold demotion mechanism is implemented or that it is exercisable
ABSENCE: no AC line reads 'the criteria_routing.json demotion path triggered by R7 metrics is implemented and documented'
findings.finding: The FP-demotion mechanism described in Success Criteria ('when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change') is a described deliverable/process with no covering Acceptance Criterion. None of the four AC lines verify that this mechanism is implemented, its threshold is tested, or that R7 instrumentation actually feeds this trigger.
findings.location: Success Criteria, bullet 2 (FP demotion paragraph); Acceptance Criteria lines 1-4
scenarios: An implementer ships R7 instrumentation but omits the automated/manual demotion path; the AC set is fully satisfied yet the described safety-valve is missing.
The 10% FP threshold is described in SC but never validated — a future high-FP criterion stays blocking because the demotion path was never built.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '[ ] The R7 dogfood monitoring pipeline produces a blocking-FP proxy metric per criterion; a worked example (or the R7 story itself) demonstrates that a criteria_routing.json advisory-demotion edit is the defined response when the proxy exceeds 10%, and docs/plan-review-gate.md names this threshold explicitly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan for `review_kernel/` changes (e.g., 'revert commit + re-run E5 gate' procedure, or an expand-contract step that keeps the old kernel path live until the new one is verified in code-review too), acknowledging the no-config-flag constraint.
evidence: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'
'incorporated DIRECTLY into the packaged review process (no config-flag or overlay gating — operator direction 2026-07-15)'
ABSENCE: no revert procedure, expand-contract sequence, or post-ship remediation path is described for review_kernel changes
findings.finding: The shared `review_kernel/` is used by both plan-review and code-review, and R5 requires changes to it; the only safeguard named is the E5 non-regression gate (pre-ship check). Given the operator's explicit no-config-flag policy, there is no described back-out/rollback plan if the kernel change causes code-review regressions after landing. The T4 criterion requires an explicit reversibility mechanism, not just a pre-ship gate.
findings.location: Context paragraph: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'; Success Criteria: 'incorporated DIRECTLY into the packaged review process (no config-flag or overlay gating)'
scenarios: R5 lands, passes E5 gate, but a subtle regression in code-review only surfaces in production; with no config flag and no rollback plan, the fix path is an unplanned emergency revert commit with no described procedure.
findings.suggested_fix: In the R5 child story AC, add a back-out clause: 'If post-land code-review regression is detected within [N] days, revert the kernel commit via Gerrit revert + re-run E5; the revert itself requires no config change.' This satisfies T4 without violating the no-flag policy.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the rollback procedure for the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump if R5 is subsequently deferred — e.g., specify whether the bump is cut only after E5 passes (not at claim time) or define how an orphaned bump segment is annotated/merged.
evidence: 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
ABSENCE: no procedure described for what happens to the version label and existing eval results if R5 is subsequently deferred/rejected after the bump is cut
findings.finding: The `IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION` bump (part of R5) is a data-shape change that permanently segments the eval corpus; the plan describes coordination to avoid two half-bumps but provides no rollback path if the bump must be reversed (e.g., R5 is deferred after the bump has already been cut). Corpus segmentation is effectively irreversible once evals have run against the new version label.
findings.location: Context paragraph: "R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)."
scenarios: R5 claims the version bump, runs E5, but E5 returns inconclusive and R5 is deferred per the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE; the corpus is now permanently split at the bump boundary with no R5 implementation to justify it, orphaning that corpus segment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit requirement in the R5 (and any kernel-touching) child story AC to audit and update any code-review documentation that references `review_kernel/` contracts when the kernel interface changes.
evidence: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'
'docs/plan-review-gate.md reflects every landed change' — scoped only to plan-review gate doc
ABSENCE: no mention of updating code-review docs/ADR if kernel interface changes
findings.finding: The plan mandates updating `docs/plan-review-gate.md` for every landed plan-review change, but does not mention updating any code-review documentation when the shared `review_kernel/` interface or behavior changes. If code-review has its own docs referencing kernel contracts, those become stale.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md reflects every landed change'; Context: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review)'
scenarios: R5 changes a kernel interface; code-review docs still reference the old behavior; a developer reading code-review docs gets incorrect information about kernel contracts.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the 10% blocking-FP proxy threshold is a named configurable parameter (e.g., a field in criteria_routing.json per criterion, or a top-level monitoring config constant) rather than a hardcoded literal in R7 instrumentation, and document where to change it.
evidence: 'when a criterion’s trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
ABSENCE: the plan does not say whether 10% is a named constant in config or a literal in R7 monitoring code
Precedent cited (calibration-3 demotion of T5e) suggests this has happened before — implying the threshold is a reusable policy that could need tuning
findings.finding: The 10% blocking-FP proxy threshold that governs automatic posture demotion (Success Criteria: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory') is stated as a fixed literal in the epic, but the plan does not specify whether this value is expressed as a named, configurable parameter (e.g., in criteria_routing.json or a monitoring config) or hardcoded in R7 instrumentation code. This threshold is plausibly expected to evolve per criterion or as the corpus matures.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
scenarios: R7 instrumentation is implemented with the 10% literal hardcoded; a future operator wants per-criterion thresholds or a different sensitivity for new detection criteria — requires a code change rather than a config edit
A new criterion added in a later epic silently inherits the same 10% threshold even if its FP characteristics warrant a different level
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `fp_proxy_demotion_threshold` field (default 0.10) to each criterion's entry in criteria_routing.json (or a top-level monitoring_config section). R7's metric-check reads this field, making per-criterion tuning a config-only change consistent with the registry-edit precedent already cited.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the R4 child (clerkish-cloggy-cod) whether the necessity/no-op probe and the lightweight bug-review tier share a single gate and are order-dependent/worthless apart, or whether they should be separate children with independent gate terms.
evidence: Title explicitly joins two distinct capabilities with '+'
A no-op probe addresses a different detection concern (redundancy/triviality) from a bug-review tier (defect-class triage)
Plan Context section does not explain why these are coupled or order-dependent
findings.finding: R4's child title bundles two potentially independently-releasable outcomes: a 'necessity/no-op probe' (detecting redundant/trivial plans) and a 'lightweight bug review tier' (triage by defect class). If these are separately testable and independently valuable, combining them in one child may obscure the boundary and complicate the gate terms.
findings.location: Child ticket: clerkish-cloggy-cod — 'R4: necessity/no-op probe + lightweight bug review tier (gated retrospective)'
scenarios: The eval gate passes for the no-op probe but not the bug-review tier (or vice versa), leaving the child in an ambiguous partial-pass state
One sub-capability proves low-value and should be deferred, but the combined child forces an all-or-nothing decision

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a structural enforcement mechanism for the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION single-bump-train constraint (e.g., a DAG dependency making R5 wait on the floor-split story's bump, or a pre-claim CI check that fails if both stories independently attempt to change the constant), not just 'claim-time coordination'.
evidence: Plan states 'coordinated at claim time' but does not specify how this coordination is enforced structurally.
sidecar.py:35: IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION = 'plan-v2' — a module constant; two stories each potentially changing it.

ADR 0036: 'Re-run per version bump — each IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION change resets the cohort; do not carry a threshold tuned on an older version into a newer one without re-running steps 3–4.' — corpus contamination is the named hazard.
The floor-split story (relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly / large-sleepful-needlefish) and R5 are named as the two concurrent bump candidates but have no DAG dependency linking them in the plan.
findings.finding: The 'one bump train, never two half-bumps' constraint on IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION is stated as a desired outcome but has no described enforcement mechanism, making it a hidden ordering/atomicity assumption. The plan says R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is 'coordinated at claim time' with the floor-split story's potential bump, but 'claim time' coordination is a social convention, not a structural gate. If both stories are worked concurrently and independently claimed, two separate bump commits can land, splitting the calibration cohort — exactly the contamination hazard ADR 0036 was designed to prevent.
findings.location: Context section: 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
scenarios: R5 and the floor-split story both land in the same release window; each author independently bumps IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION (one to plan-v3, the other to plan-v3 or plan-v4); sidecars produced by the two formulas are pooled under the same version tag, defeating the segmented calibration replay.

R5 is claimed first and bumps the constant; the floor-split story is claimed later and, not knowing R5 already bumped it, bumps it again — producing two cohort resets for one logical change, losing accumulated field data.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit ordering dependency: R5 must sequence AFTER the floor-split story resolves its own IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump (or explicitly records 'no bump needed'), and document this as a DAG edge in R5's child AC rather than a prose coordination note in the epic context.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'LangChain MapReduce / LlamaIndex map_reduce pattern' reference in docs/plan-review-gate.md to accurately describe the hand-rolled token-budget chunker in review_kernel/verify.py, removing the unverified assertion that these third-party frameworks are used.
evidence: Plan text: 'The chunking is encapsulated inside the verify step (the LangChain MapReduce / LlamaIndex map_reduce pattern), not exposed as a workflow fan-out'
Codebase search for 'LangChain' and 'LlamaIndex': zero matches in entire repository
Codebase search for 'map_reduce': zero matches
Actual implementation in review_kernel/verify.py uses hand-rolled verify_request_chunks() with no external framework dependency
R5 in the plan requires changes to review_kernel/ — a future implementer reading this description may expect or introduce LangChain/LlamaIndex as a dependency when none is needed
findings.finding: The plan's documentation (docs/plan-review-gate.md) asserts that the verify token-budget chunking is implemented using 'the LangChain MapReduce / LlamaIndex map_reduce pattern' — but neither LangChain nor LlamaIndex appears anywhere in the codebase. The actual implementation (review_kernel/verify.py:verify_findings + verify_request_chunks) is a hand-rolled token-budget chunker with no such external dependency. This is an unverified capability assertion that names third-party libraries the system does not use, which could mislead future R5 implementers about what infrastructure is available.
findings.location: docs/plan-review-gate.md, 'Verify token-budget chunking' section (line ~103); cross-referenced against src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py
scenarios: An implementer working on R5 (kernel sub-answer changes) reads the docs and believes LangChain or LlamaIndex is a required or available dependency, introducing an unintended third-party library.
A reviewer of R5's Gerrit change flags the absence of LangChain as a missing dependency rather than recognizing it as a misleading documentation analogy.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the parenthetical with a description of the actual pattern, e.g.: '(a hand-rolled token-budget split: the findings are partitioned into chunks whose estimated token count fits within `floor(window × headroom)`, each verified in a separate call, then re-merged by global index)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` is accessible at the path the plan names (or update the path), and that commit `143d5074e` is reachable — the child story gate terms cannot be authored without it.
evidence: Plan text: 'Integrate the task-decomposition SOTA research (docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md, commit 143d5074e, ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b)'
Plan text: 'each child R1-R7 story carries its own falsifiable pass/fail thresholds (drawn from the report's section-6 experiment table) INSIDE its Acceptance Criteria'
Plan text: 'The report's §5 mined this store's own history: post-claim description edits on 16/505 claimed work tickets (3.2%)'
Directory listing: `docs/research/` does not exist in the repo — Contradicted evidence class
Ticket lookup for englacial-communal-conure/d39b: 'Ticket not found' — Contradicted evidence class
findings.finding: The SOTA research document `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` (commit 143d5074e, ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b) is the authoritative source for all R1–R7 gate terms: the plan explicitly states 'child ACs are the authoritative gate rows' drawn from 'the report's section-6 experiment table' and '§5 mined this store's own history'. The `docs/research/` directory does not exist in the repo, making this artifact absent (Contradicted). Child story authors cannot verify the falsifiable thresholds, experiment numbering (E1/E2/E3/E5/E6), or historical mining figures (3.2% edit rate, 3/8 miss identification) that the plan defers to as the source of truth for every gate row.
findings.location: Context section: 'docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md, commit 143d5074e, ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b'; also 'the report's section-6 experiment table' and '§5 mined this store's own history'
scenarios: A child story author goes to author R1's AC gate terms referencing 'the report's section-6 experiment table' and finds no document to consult — gate thresholds become unverifiable assertions.
E1 inter-rater kappa threshold (>= 0.7, cited in AC) cannot be traced to the source experiment table, undermining the falsifiability guarantee.
The historical mining figures (3/8 miss identification, binomial CI 9–76%) cited in Context cannot be audited, leaving the rationale for child E1 re-verification ungrounded.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify the document's actual committed path (`git show 143d5074e --name-only` or `git log --all --full-history -- 'docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md'`). If it lives under a different path, update the plan reference. If it was never committed to this repo, commit it (or a permalink to it) before authoring any R1–R7 child stories.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'blocking-FP proxy' computation: specify the metric formula (e.g. blocked_plans_overridden / total_plans_blocked_by_criterion over a trailing N-review window), the data source (REVIEW_RESULT sidecar fields or a dedicated R7 counter), and the window size — co-located with the demotion rule in either the R7 child AC or a docs/plan-review-gate.md addendum.
evidence: Success Criteria quote: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
Codebase search for 'blocking_fp_proxy', 'fp_rate', 'trailing_window' returns zero matches
sidecar.py (IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION='plan-v2') emits per-criterion verdicts but contains no FP-proxy computation or field
R7 is described as providing dogfood monitoring but the plan does not define what R7 actually instruments or what metrics it emits
findings.finding: The 'blocking-FP proxy' metric is used as a named operational decision trigger with a concrete 10% threshold that causes an automated `criteria_routing.json` posture demotion, but the metric is never defined: no specification of what numerator/denominator it uses, what trailing window it measures, or what data source (REVIEW_RESULT events? sidecar fields? a manual audit?) drives the computation. A search of the entire codebase finds no implementation of `blocking_fp`, `fp_proxy`, `trailing_fp`, or `trailing_window` — the metric does not yet exist.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'; also AC4
scenarios: An executor implementing R7 needs to know what to instrument: what event fields, what windowing logic, and what query produces the 10% threshold — none of this is specified, so R7 can ship something that never actually triggers the demotion rule.
A criterion legitimately over-firing (e.g. T5e after calibration-3) needs the proxy to fire the demotion, but if the proxy is not computed, the demotion mechanism silently fails.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the R7 child's Acceptance Criteria (or to docs/plan-review-gate.md): 'blocking-FP proxy for criterion C = (# reviews where C was the sole blocker AND the author overrode or removed the finding) / (# reviews where C produced a blocking verdict), trailing N=20 reviews. R7 must emit a per-criterion `blocked_overridden` counter in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar so the proxy is computable offline.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the actor and trigger mechanism for the blocking-FP proxy demotion: state whether R7 emits an automated alert, whether a human makes the criteria_routing.json edit on review, and whether re-promotion (advisory → blocking) requires a passing eval re-run — co-locate this decision rule with the criteria_routing.json schema description or in the R7 child ACs.
evidence: Success Criteria names the demotion rule but does not specify the actor: 'its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change' — passive voice, actor unspecified
criteria_routing.json defines `default_posture` as a static field with no associated demotion-trigger metadata (tool-verified: no `fp_threshold` or `promotion_policy` field in any entry)

The plan references 'precedent: calibration 3 demoted T5e to advisory on FP evidence' — confirming this has been done manually before, but the plan does not codify whether R7 automates it or keeps it manual
findings.finding: The posture-demotion protocol (blocking-FP proxy >10% → demote `default_posture` in `criteria_routing.json`) is stated in the Success Criteria section but is not co-located with the schema it drives (`criteria_routing.json` and the `default_posture` field). The rule is unidirectional (it names 'demotion' but not 're-promotion'), and neither the triggering computation (see above) nor the actor that executes the demotion (automated script? manual human decision? CI job?) is specified.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'new criteria enter at advisory POSTURE first... with R7 dogfood monitoring: when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
scenarios: After R7 ships, the blocking-FP proxy for a new criterion exceeds 10% but no one knows whose job it is to edit criteria_routing.json or when to do so — the safety valve is never pulled.
An automated job performs the demotion without human review, potentially demoting a sound criterion based on a noisy short window.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Restate the A/B prohibition with its scope at the point it is introduced: replace 'no A/B testing' with 'no in-situ (live-user) A/B testing — offline eval comparisons (E1–E3, E5–E6) are the validation method' so the executor does not conflate the two.
evidence: First paragraph: 'no A/B testing - validation is eval-based (the E-gates below)'
Context: 'E4 (the in-situ A/B experiment) is deliberately DROPPED per the operator's no-A/B directive, which is why the experiment set is E1/E2/E3/E5/E6'
The distinction between in-situ A/B testing (dropped) and offline eval comparisons (kept) is stated in the Context but not surfaced at the point the prohibition is introduced
findings.finding: The plan introduces the named phrase 'no A/B testing' twice in prominent positions (title paragraph and Context section), and the phrase 'per operator direction (2026-07-15): no A/B testing' is prominently stated. This is a classic instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern: stating a prohibition by naming the prohibited thing draws attention to it. An executor implementing experiments E1–E6 who reads 'no A/B testing' twice at the top may interpret their eval harness design as needing to avoid anything resembling A/B splits, even where controlled eval comparisons (not user-facing A/B tests) are the correct tool. The plan does not distinguish 'in-situ A/B testing on live users' (dropped as E4) from 'controlled eval comparisons' (E1–E3, E5–E6).
findings.location: Plan title paragraph: 'Per operator direction (2026-07-15): no A/B testing'; Context section: 'E4 (the in-situ A/B experiment) is deliberately DROPPED per the operator's no-A/B directive'
scenarios: An executor designing E2 or E3 eval harnesses avoids any head-to-head comparison design because they read 'no A/B testing' as a blanket prohibition, producing weaker eval designs.
The prohibition is correctly scoped to in-situ/live-user experiments but stated globally, causing confusion when the child R-stories are being scoped.
findings.suggested_fix: In the first paragraph, change 'no A/B testing - validation is eval-based' to 'no in-situ A/B testing on live reviews — offline eval comparisons (E1–E3, E5–E6) are the validation method'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add E2 (d125-b4ce) as an explicit prerequisite in R4's Scope/sequencing note and confirm E2 class-4 mutants exist before the retrospective gate is evaluated.
evidence: R4 AC1: 'the necessity probe fires on the 5886 plan fixture (pinned as a test) and on >=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants with <=1/10 controls flagged'
R4 Scope: 'Sequenced AFTER the floor-split story to avoid routing/kernel conflicts' — no mention of E2
findings.finding: R4 gates on >=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants in its retrospective AC but names only the floor-split story as a sequencing predecessor — E2 (child d125) must be complete before R4's retrospective gate is evaluable, yet E2 is not listed as a dependency.
findings.location: child 03a9-e6d1-4acc-435d (R4), Acceptance Criteria line 1 and Scope sequencing note
scenarios: R4 is picked up after the floor-split story ships but while E2 is still in progress; the author reaches the retrospective gate AC and finds the class-4 mutant corpus does not yet exist, causing rework or a gate mis-evaluation.
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'Sequenced AFTER E2 (d125-b4ce) for the class-4 unnecessary-mechanism mutant corpus' to R4's Scope sequencing section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC to R3 stating that the packaged rubric must be the E6-committed draft (or a documented correction thereof), and add E6 (a880) as a sequencing dependency in R3's Scope.
evidence: E6 What: 'the CANDIDATE rubric draft is authored HERE as an experiment artifact (committed under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/, not the packaged reviewers/ tree), so no circular dependency on R3 - R3 later lands the draft, corrected per these results, as the packaged rubric'

R3 AC1: 'Gate-row evidence committed before landing: >=7/10 E2 layer-cake/ordering mutants detected, <=1/10 controls flagged, E6 order-shuffle stability recorded for the rubric' — mentions E6 results but not the E6-produced rubric draft artifact
R3 Scope: no mention of consuming the E6 experiment-branch rubric artifact
findings.finding: R3 depends on E6 producing the candidate rubric draft (committed as an experiment artifact) but names E6 only as a source of gate-row evidence in its ACs — the rubric draft handoff is undocumented in R3, creating an owned-by-none artifact gap.
findings.location: child 3a27-f8fa-c417-43db (R3), Acceptance Criteria and Scope; child a880-b7e1-dc3e-407c (E6), What section
scenarios: R3 implementer treats E6 as purely a numeric gate check (kappa, stability counts) and authors a new rubric from scratch, diverging from the E6-tested draft; the packaged rubric is never validated by E6's order-shuffle run.
E6 finds moderate instability in the draft and records it as a note rather than a hard fail; R3 has no AC step requiring the draft to be corrected before landing.
findings.suggested_fix: In R3 AC1, add: 'The rubric text landed is traceable to the E6 experiment-branch draft (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/); any corrections relative to the draft are recorded in the gate writeup.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add E1 (e95e) as an explicit sequencing prerequisite in R7's Scope section to enforce the parent's 'after E1' ordering rule.
evidence: R7 AC2: 'the job classifies the 8 §5.2 tickets with >=6/8 agreement against the E1 frozen labels, recorded in the writeup'
R7 Scope: 'docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ (instrumentation job + README documenting metrics, alarm rule, and cadence)' — no mention of E1 dependency
Parent Context: 'first wave after E1 is R7 + R6 (instrumentation and the no-gate move)'
findings.finding: R7's validation gate requires the E1-frozen §5.2 labels ('>=6/8 agreement against the E1 frozen labels'), but R7's Scope/sequencing note does not name E1 as a prerequisite — the parent's ordering rule ('first wave after E1 is R7+R6') is not captured within R7 itself.
findings.location: child b1a4-af7a-4a48-465d (R7), Acceptance Criteria line 2 and Scope
scenarios: R7 is started before E1's labels are committed; the validation gate references labels that don't exist yet, or the author uses informal/pre-freeze label estimates that differ from the final E1 corpus, producing an unverifiable agreement number.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to R7 Scope: 'Sequenced AFTER E1 (e95e-1543) so that the frozen §5.2 labels exist before the reproduction gate is evaluated.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add E1 (e95e) as an explicit sequencing prerequisite in R2's Scope sequencing note.
evidence: R2 What: 'run the lexicon over the full historical AC corpus (from E1's outcome corpus tickets)'
R2 Scope: 'Sequenced AFTER the floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) to avoid criteria_routing.json conflicts' — no mention of E1
R2 AC1: 'The lexicon eval over the full historical AC corpus is committed BEFORE the lint lands' — corpus sourced from E1
findings.finding: R2 runs its lexicon eval 'over the full historical AC corpus (from E1's outcome corpus tickets)' but names only the floor-split story as a sequencing predecessor — E1 must be committed first for the corpus to exist, yet E1 is not listed as a dependency in R2.
findings.location: child b080-fa0b-b1ce-4fad (R2), What section and Scope sequencing note
scenarios: R2 is started after the floor-split story but before E1 is complete; the author runs the lexicon eval against an informal or incomplete AC corpus rather than E1's committed outcome corpus, producing non-reproducible precision/flag-rate numbers.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to R2 Scope: 'Sequenced AFTER E1 (e95e-1543) so the full historical AC corpus (outcome corpus tickets) is available for the lexicon eval.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document in E5's Scope (or R5's AC) the handoff convention: which branch/artifact E5 consumes, who authors it, and that the E5 replay must be re-run if R5's candidate changes after E5 runs.
evidence: E5 Scope: 'Reads src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (candidate branch build); no packaged changes land from this story'
E5 What: 'run the R5-amended kernel (asserted_capability_confirmed sub-answer added)'
R5 AC1: 'Gate-row evidence committed before landing: E5 byte-identical replay artifact'
Neither child names a branch, an artifact path, or a step where R5 authors the candidate build for E5 to consume
findings.finding: E5 requires a 'candidate branch build' of the amended review_kernel (R5's change) to replay, but neither E5 nor R5 documents the handoff — branch naming convention, artifact location, or who authors the candidate build before E5 runs — leaving the E5/R5 dependency implicit and unverifiable.
findings.location: child 5342-568c-b881-4b2a (E5), Scope; child ea39-c2d1-e26a-4ee6 (R5), Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: E5 implementer is ready to run the replay but R5's candidate change hasn't been pushed to any agreed branch; E5 either blocks waiting or runs against the wrong build.

R5 implementer pushes a preliminary version of the kernel change; E5 runs against it and produces a byte-identical diff; R5 subsequently amends the change; the E5 artifact no longer reflects the actually-landed R5 change.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to E5 Scope: 'Consumes the R5 candidate branch (ea39-c2d1); R5 must tag a stable candidate commit before E5 runs; if R5 amends the kernel after E5 completes, E5 must re-run before R5 can claim the gate row.'

findings.finding: The Success Criteria reference a 'demote-on-alarm' protocol ('alarm at trailing FP >10%, demote-on-alarm precedent T3/T10') but neither T3 nor T10 have a demote-on-alarm protocol defined in their `criteria_routing.json` entries or reviewer prompts, and the operational meaning of 'demote' (flip `default_posture`? lower `block_threshold`? disable criterion?) is never specified anywhere in the plan or linked files.
findings.location: Success Criteria second bullet: 'alarm at trailing FP >10%, demote-on-alarm precedent T3/T10'

findings.finding: R6 (child 5dac-c177-56ff-4af4) ships without an eval gate row, yet parent SC1 requires 'every implemented recommendation has a committed docs/experiments/ writeup showing its gate row passed before implementation' and parent AC3 requires each recommendation 'landed through Gerrit with its gate row demonstrably passed … no recommendation implemented without its gate.' R6 has no gate row and no gate-row writeup, referencing only an 'epic gate table' that is not defined within the plan text or any child — the exemption is unverifiable from the plan as written.
findings.location: child 5dac-c177-56ff-4af4 (R6)
findings.suggested_fix: Commit a one-page docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/r6-gate-rationale.md that cites the gate-table row, records the 'no detection risk / coaching-only' classification, and is referenced in R6's ACs so AC3 can be closed by inspection.

findings.finding: The plan's AC3 and Success Criteria refer to a 'gate row' that must be 'demonstrably passed' before each R1-R7 implementation, and to a 'gate table' that dictates advisory posture for new criteria — but neither 'gate row' nor 'gate table' is defined as a schema anywhere in the plan text or linked files. An executor implementing R1-R7 cannot know what fields a gate row must contain (pass/fail condition, metric value, corpus reference, date?) to count as 'demonstrably passed.'
findings.location: Success Criteria (second bullet) and AC3: 'gate row demonstrably passed'; Success Criteria: 'where the gate table says so'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/gate-table.md` with the gate-row schema (fields: `experiment_id`, `metric`, `observed`, `threshold`, `pass`, `corpus_ref`, `date`) and reference it from AC3. Each R-child's writeup must include a populated gate-row block.

findings.finding: AC2 requires results to be 'recorded as ticket comments', which is an external-system artifact (issue/ticket tracker), not a repo or CI artifact. This criterion is not tagged [operator-attested] (ADR 0043), so it cannot be verified in-session via repo artifacts or a deterministic command — an agent closing this story cannot confirm the AC is met without accessing the external ticket system.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC line 2: '…results recorded as ticket comments AND a docs/experiments/ writeup'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'results recorded as ticket comments AND a docs/experiments/ writeup' to 'results recorded in the docs/experiments/ writeup (a RESULTS section listing gate row pass/fail outcomes)' — this makes the AC fully in-repo verifiable and removes the external-system dependency.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
